title: Warren Hamilton Wins!
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States celebrates abolitionist Warren Hamilton's general election victory in Oklahoma's Senate District 7 race over Democrat Jerry Donathan, framing it as a decisive blow to anti-abolitionist Republicans and Democrats and a launching point for a forthcoming bill to abolish abortion in Oklahoma.
audiences: Political journalist covering state legislative races

questions.question: What were the final vote percentages in the Senate District 7 general election?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering state legislative races
questions.answer: Warren Hamilton won with 72.17 percent of the vote, while Democrat Jerry Donathan received 27.83 percent.
questions.quote: The Democrat in the Senate District 7 race Jerry Donathan garnered only 27.83 percent of the vote while Hamilton received a stunningly large 72.17 percent.

questions.question: Why does Free the States consider Warren Hamilton a trustworthy legislator for the abolitionist cause?
questions.answer: Hamilton and his wife Sherrie have been active in the Abolitionist Movement since 2018, and the article presents him as someone who can be trusted to author and fight for a bill of total and immediate abolition.
questions.quote: He is someone the movement can trust to author a bill of total and immediate abolition and fight valiantly for its passage.

questions.question: How did the defeated Republican incumbent Larry Boggs respond after losing the primary?
questions.answer: Boggs, who did not support immediate abolition, threw his support to the pro-abortion Democrat Jerry Donathan after losing the primary to Hamilton.
questions.quote: After being defeated, [Boggs threw his support to Donathan](https://www.facebook.com/DonathanForSenate/posts/178538857177436), the pro-abortion Democrat.

questions.question: What did Holly Gerard claim about electability when she backed Boggs in the primary?
questions.answer: Gerard told Hamilton campaign volunteer Kyle Brown that she supported Boggs because he was the only Republican who could win the general election, an assessment Hamilton's landslide subsequently disproved.
questions.quote: She told Hamilton campaign volunteer Kyle Brown that she supported Boggs because he was the only Republican who could win the general election.

questions.question: By how much did Hamilton outperform Boggs's 2016 general election numbers?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering state legislative races
questions.answer: Hamilton outperformed Boggs's 2016 general election totals by more than 16 points; Boggs had only managed 55.75 percent against the Democrat in 2016.
questions.quote: Gerard's understanding of Oklahoma politics was proven embarrassingly wrong by Hamilton's victory as he outperformed Boggs' 2016 general election totals by more than 16 points.

questions.question: What was the central issue that defined the Republican primary between Hamilton and Boggs?
questions.answer: The defining debate of the race was whether abortion should be immediately abolished, with Boggs opposing immediate abolition and Hamilton championing it.
questions.quote: Boggs did not support the abolition of abortion, and whether abortion should be immediately abolished was the defining debate of the race.

questions.question: What does Free the States plan to do legislatively as a result of Hamilton's win?
questions.answer: The organization plans to introduce a bill to abolish abortion in Oklahoma the following year and is preparing to mobilize Oklahomans to support its passage.
questions.quote: There will be a bill to abolish abortion in Oklahoma next year and we are gearing up to rally the people of Oklahoma to support it.

questions.question: How does the article characterize the political significance of Hamilton's victory beyond a single Senate seat?
questions.answer: It frames the victory as a massive win for the Abolitionist Movement and a simultaneous defeat for both anti-abolitionist Democrats and anti-abolitionist Republicans across Oklahoma.
questions.quote: Hamilton's election to the Oklahoma State Senate is a massive victory for the Abolitionist Movement and a massive defeat for anti-abolitionist Democrats and Republicans in Oklahoma.
